ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301116 covers tiramisu as a dessert category. This record is a branded Starbucks variant with coffee-infused sponge and mascarpone; no exact branded FoodOn entry exists. 'Exact' match type used as the general food item 'tiramisu' is an exact match.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0EJX covers tiramisu as a composite dessert. Branded Starbucks variant not separately coded; 'exact' match type used as the general food item 'tiramisu' is an exact match.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for tiramisu cake or this branded product. Null applied.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard slice of Starbucks Coffee Tiramisu Cake weighs approximately 120 g, consistent with typical Starbucks Singapore cake slice portions.

nutrient_source.title: Starbucks Coffee Tiramisu Cake — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Starbucks Coffee Tiramisu Cake (cr_id G00010286). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Saturated fat is estimated from mascarpone and cream composition using USDA FoodData Central reference data for mascarpone (FDC ID 173420) and heavy whipping cream (FDC ID 170859) as a supplementary cross-check, as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Starbucks Coffee Tiramisu Cake. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on the closest analogous composite dessert: sponge cake with cream filling, which has a reported GI of approximately 60–70 in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. The high sugar content (18.7 g/100g) and low fibre (0.6 g/100g) support a medium-to-high GI estimate; 65 is used as a conservative central estimate. Glycemic load per 120 g serving = (65 × 29.44 × 1.20) / 100 = 23.1 g carbohydrate per serving × 65 / 100 ≈ 23.0 × 0.65 = 14.9; recalculated as (65 × 35.33) / 100 = 22.96 rounded to 23.0. Note: GL per serving (120 g) = 0.65 × 35.33 = 22.96 ≈ 23.0. Value in field set to 23.0.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (18.7 g/100g) with low dietary fibre (0.6 g/100g) and estimated saturated fat ~12.5 g/100g
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this item: the high free-sugar load (18.7 g/100g) independently elevates blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidelines, and the high saturated fat content (~12.5 g/100g from mascarpone and chantilly cream) contributes to arterial stiffness; sodium at 173 mg/100g is moderate but does not offset the sugar and fat burden.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar 18.7 g/100g exceeding the 15 g/100g diabetes threshold with dietary fibre only 0.6 g/100g
type2_diabetes.guidance: This cake is unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes: the sugar content of 18.7 g/100g with negligible fibre (0.6 g/100g) produces a rapid postprandial glucose spike, and the estimated GI of 65 with a glycemic load of ~23 per 120 g slice makes portion control extremely difficult; avoidance is strongly recommended.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~12.5 g/100g, cholesterol 121 mg/100g, and high free-sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this product: the estimated saturated fat of ~12.5 g/100g (primarily from mascarpone and chantilly cream) exceeds the AHA recommended daily limit in a single serving, cholesterol at 121 mg/100g is clinically significant, and the high sugar content (18.7 g/100g) further elevates triglyceride and LDL risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~12.5 g/100g and high sugar (18.7 g/100g) promoting pro-inflammatory cytokine activity
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this item: the high saturated fat from mascarpone and cream (~12.5 g/100g) exceeds the RA threshold of 5 g/100g and promotes TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory cytokine activity, which is further amplified by the high free-sugar content of 18.7 g/100g.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Starbucks Coffee Tiramisu Cake is a branded chilled dessert cake sold at Starbucks Singapore outlets. It consists of layers of soft coffee-infused sponge cake soaked in Starbucks espresso, alternating with velvety mascarpone cheese cream, topped with chantilly whipped cream and dusted with cocoa powder. The texture is moist and creamy with a pronounced coffee bitterness balanced by the rich sweetness of mascarpone and chantilly cream. Per 100 g, the cake provides 353 kcal, 23.86 g fat (estimated saturated fat ~12.5 g from mascarpone and cream), 29.44 g carbohydrates, 18.7 g sugar, 5.65 g protein, 0.6 g dietary fibre, and 173 mg sodium. The high sugar content (18.7 g/100g) with very low fibre (0.6 g/100g) makes this item clinically unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The estimated glycemic index of 65 (medium) and glycemic load of approximately 23 per 120 g slice indicate a significant postprandial glucose response. The product is vegetarian but contains gluten, dairy, and eggs. It is classified as ultra-processed and is best consumed as an occasional treat in small portions by healthy adults.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Starbucks tiramisu cake?
Is Starbucks coffee tiramisu cake suitable for diabetics?
Starbucks tiramisu cake nutrition facts Singapore
How much sugar in Starbucks tiramisu cake?
Can I eat tiramisu cake if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010286), which is an approved primary source for Singapore food products. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central reference data for mascarpone and heavy cream as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item; this single estimated field reduces confidence from absolute certainty but the overall record remains high confidence given the exact HPB match for all other nutrients.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (12.5 g/100g) is an estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central reference values for mascarpone cheese (FDC ID 173420, ~25 g sat fat/100g) and heavy whipping cream (FDC ID 170859, ~23 g sat fat/100g), weighted proportionally against the known fat total of 23.86 g/100g. This estimate is documented as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this product. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin profile, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and moisture data are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this product and are not available in USDA Branded Foods for this specific Starbucks Singapore item as of 2026-04-16. 3. Glycemic index of 65 is an analogous estimate based on sponge cake with cream filling from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables; no direct GI measurement for this branded product was found. Glycemic load per 120 g serving = 0.65 × (29.44 × 1.20) = 0.65 × 35.33 = 23.0. 4. food_group corrected from input value 'Beverages / Coffee' to 'Desserts / Cakes and pastries' as this is a solid cake product, not a beverage. 5. Halal status not confirmed for this Starbucks Singapore product; is_halal set to false pending official Starbucks Singapore halal certification verification.
